prompt_text: Workable Slack integration: how does it work?
response:
Workable’s native Slack integration sends **recruiting notifications straight into Slack** and lets you take **basic hiring actions directly from Slack** (commenting, replying to SMS, and approving/rejecting certain requests), once a Workable admin connects a single Slack workspace to the account.[1][7]

Here’s how it works in practice:

**1. One-time setup (admin)**
- A **Workable admin** goes to **Settings → Integrations → Apps** in Workable and finds **“Integration with Slack.”**[1]  
- They click **Add to Slack**, sign in to the correct Slack workspace, and grant the requested permissions.[1][2][7]  
- After that, Slack becomes the active workspace for that Workable account (note: **one Workable account ↔ one Slack workspace only**, and the same Slack workspace can’t be linked to multiple Workable accounts).[1]

**2. User-level notification preferences**
- Each user controls their own Slack alerts under **Your Preferences** in Workable.[1]  
- Users can turn specific **Slack notifications on/off** (e.g., new applicants, comments, evaluations, approval requests).[1][6][7]  
- Default Slack settings are based on each user’s email notification settings but can be customized.[1]  
- The **email address in Workable must match the email address in Slack** for that user to receive notifications.[1]

**3. What you see in Slack**
Workable posts messages into Slack channels or DMs (depending on your configuration) for events such as:[1][6][7]  
- **New applicants** for a job  
- **New comments** on candidates  
- **New evaluations** by colleagues  
- **Offer and requisition approval requests**  
- Other hiring workflow events configured in your preferences  

Notifications are interactive: they show **candidate name, job title, and key context**, and clicking the candidate or job link takes you to the corresponding page in Workable for full details.[1]

**4. Actions you can take directly in Slack**
From Workable’s Slack messages you can perform certain actions without opening Workable:[1][7]  
- **Reply to @‑mention comments** (continue a discussion from Slack)  
- **Add new comments** on candidates from a comment notification  
- **Reply to SMS messages** sent to candidates  
- **Approve or reject requisition approval requests** (single or bulk)  
- **Approve or reject offer approval requests** (if you are not a signatory; signatories are redirected to Workable to approve & sign)[1]  

When commenting from Slack, the **visibility of your comment** inherits the previous comment’s visibility by default, but you can change it in Slack before sending.[1]

**5. Disabling or changing the integration**
- A Workable admin can **disconnect Slack** in **Settings → Integrations → Apps → Integration with Slack → Disconnect.**[1]  
- If an admin belongs to multiple Workable accounts, Slack can only be enabled on **one** of those accounts by that admin; another admin must connect Slack for any other account.[1]

**6. Alternative/extended integrations**
Beyond the native integration, you can also connect Workable and Slack via automation tools if you need custom workflows:  
- **Zapier**: Build “Zaps” where a **trigger** in one app (e.g., “New candidate in Workable”) causes an **action** in the other (e.g., “Post message in Slack” or “Add Comment Candidate” in Workable).[3][4]  
- Other iPaaS tools (e.g., LeadsBridge, Workload) offer similar Slack–Workable automation options.[5][8]

If you tell me your role (e.g., recruiter, hiring manager, HR admin) and what you want to see in Slack (e.g., only approvals, all applicants, per-job channels), I can outline an exact configuration.
